step_by_step_thinking:
An imaginary occlusal curve that contacts the buccal and lingual cusp tips of mandibular buccal teeth is called the **Curve of Spee**.
Therefore, the correct option is \boxed{B}. The information states explicitly in the text: "[...] The occlusal curve is called the Curve of Spee." Hence, this matches exactly with our requirement to identify the term describing the contact made by both buccal and lingual cusps on the mandibular teeth.
\boxed{B} Based on the detailed description given about the occlusal anatomy and characteristics of specific curves related to the mandibular posterior region, specifically mentioning how the Curve of Spee describes the contact involving both buccal and lingual cusp tips, my conclusion supports selecting Option B.
